上瘾是指尽管一种化学物质正在造成伤害，但它无法停止消费或从事某种活动。
我几乎接触到了所有与上瘾有关的物质或行为：酒精、毒品、咖啡、色情、性、赌博、工作、消费、设备和社交媒体。我都滥用过，但别以为我上瘾了。在平衡记分卡上，这些物质和行为，包括虐待和所有，在我的生活中都是净积极的，甚至@Twitter。
我们物种的大多数疾病和困难都是由稀缺造成的-盐、糖、脂肪太少，认可太少，安全，交配机会太少。因此，当我们发现这些东西时，我们的大脑就会产生终极的奖赏--愉悦荷尔蒙多巴胺。这是有道理的。大自然奖励那些确保物种繁殖的行为。
流水线、处理能力和亚马逊Prime不仅达到了生存的最低门槛，而且给我们的物种带来了新的威胁：超多。糖尿病、收入不平等和假新闻都是我们相信越多越好的结果。杰夫·贝佐斯捕获和囤积挪威的GDP对这个物种来说没有意义，但他的本能(害怕饥饿，挥舞着权力)占据着至高无上的地位。
生存、繁殖和消费应该使下一代变得更聪明、更快、更强大。事情脱轨的地方是我们的创新经济比我们的本能发展得更快的一个功能。从历史上看，人类从事的活动都有自然的停止提示-章节的结尾，结尾的字幕。Facebook、Instagram和Netflix等平台已经系统地消除了停车提示。甚至赌场的布局也刻意没有硬角，所以这都是一个连续的空间，你会一直穿过它，继续到下一场比赛。
技术进步，对我们本能的校准，在无穷无尽的卷轴中达到顶峰。我们找不到开关。与我们的父母和祖父母不同，对我们来说，多巴胺的释放不再依赖于牺牲、承诺或毅力，而是静静地坐着，就像在15秒、14秒、13秒之后，第五集就要开始了。过滤过的照片更多了，色情作品更多了，股票更多了，利润更多了，多巴也更多了，更多的时间没有了需要从事生活的麻烦。
最近的顶级交易商是在线交易平台(OTP)。在交易平台上，无穷无尽的卷轴是什么样子的？
游戏化：用户每天最多可以点击1000次，以提高自己在罗宾汉现金管理功能(本质上是应用程序上的一个高收益支票账户)等待名单上的位置。
我们的机构(法院、国会、证券交易委员会)应该放慢我们的思考速度，这样我们的本能就会得到遏制，我们就可以决定不歧视，不往河里倒汞，不让一家破产的汽车租赁公司(Hertz)发行注定一文不值的股票。你输了，他们就赢了。
技术变革的速度远远超过了我们人类适应无穷无尽的刺激的能力。这种调制方式上的差异爆发了我们青少年抑郁和社会混乱的程度。我们正处在一场超级海洋喷火中，每天都在加速，希望机身能在接近音障时保持一致--播放“辛普森一家”31季，栩栩如生的视频游戏，无处不在的日益极端的色情内容，高清实时记录你15岁的女儿没有被邀请参加的派对，社交媒体算法推动情绪与真实性的对比，以及立即批准“公牛扩散”的保证金。
昨天，在得知20岁的亚历山大·卡恩斯(Alexander Kearns)自杀的消息后，我简直是一团糟。卡恩斯来自伊利诺伊州内珀维尔，对市场感兴趣，开始交易股票。卡恩斯错误地认为，在罗宾汉应用程序上交易期权后，他下跌了73万美元，并结束了自己的生命。我们不知道还有什么因素在这里起作用，年轻人在市场上赔钱后自杀并不是什么新现象。
Facebook和Twitter做了CNN和福克斯几十年来一直在做的事情，但做得更好。我担心罗宾汉可能会成为一个让人上瘾的交易平台--Instagram。罗宾汉的用户年龄偏小(32%的访问者年龄在25-34岁之间)。该公司报告称，2020年第一季度新增客户300万。一半是首次交易。此外，随着拉斯维加斯和体育博彩几乎关闭，OTPS已经成为新兴赌博成瘾扎根的地方和/或你的赞助商是经销商的康复设施。
学习投资和了解市场是件好事，与在线朋友…联系也是如此。在某种程度上。社交媒体和赌博有相同的上瘾心理机制：可变奖励(当你不断执行一个动作，希望获得可能但不太可能的奖励)。这是一种“最容易上瘾、最难戒掉”的行为。罗宾汉的管理层和投资者已经从大技术公司得到了启示，并做出了一个有意识的决定，为了个人财富而无视我们年轻人的福祉。
12%的交易活动来自日内交易者，而日内交易者只占所有有利可图的交易者的1.6%。
投资者在其投资组合中持有风险较高的股票，他们目前的经济状况与他们的抱负水平之间存在很大差异。
大多数文章将集中在我们美国人认为的新手在线交易员…激增带来的深刻风险上。市场可能会下跌。大多数市场高点都与散户投资者入场不谋而合。据我所知，我们还没有见过20多岁的人投资政府救援基金，通过智能手机的预先批准来杠杆化，导致市场崩盘的规模。
我们的民选官员以及对金钱和创新者的粗暴崇拜已经淹没了那些负责减缓我们思维速度和保护我们孩子安全的机构。乔·斯卡伯勒(Joe Scarborough)说得好：“马克、谢丽尔和杰克，你们暴露了自己是平淡无奇的庸俗之徒，将美国人的健康、种族正义、公平选举和基本真理置于危险境地。”
我们在哪里转弯？保护孩子免受设备成瘾的压力主要落在父母身上--限制(严重)使用，并让学校的其他家长也限制使用，这样孩子们就不会觉得自己是一个例外。这很困难，而且需要完成。“电子速食”，也许对整个家庭来说，可以让神经系统重新启动。降低你的多巴胺阈值会让你获得更少的快感。
上瘾的威胁一直在拖累我们的家庭。我们的一个儿子表现出了与设备成瘾一致的行为。太可怕了。他做的、说的和努力的每一件事，都是为了追求在他的iPad上等待着的多巴热销。他妈妈和我正在做大多数父母都会做的事情--阅读，寻求外界帮助，限制使用。但最重要的是，我们在试着放慢脚步。花时间陪他，尤其是在户外或看书。和他一起上床的时间到了，给他讲他祖父成为皇家海军蛙人的故事。让一切都慢下来。它似乎在起作用。
我看到了亚历克斯·卡恩斯，也看到了我的大儿子。一个笑容满面的书呆子，对市场着迷，寻找多巴安非他明。我无法想象那个家庭的痛苦。我无法想象我们如何失去了剧本，让有意义的-创新和金钱-压倒了深刻的东西：我们的孩子。青少年自杀率在十年间增加了56%。2009年至2015年间，10岁至14岁的女孩自残事件增加了两倍。每天使用社交媒体超过五个小时的青少年患抑郁症的可能性是那些每天使用社交媒体不到一小时的青少年的两倍。
难怪蒂姆·库克不想让他的侄子出现在社交媒体上？如果他不是蒂姆·库克，他还会说，我也不想让他有iPad吗？
武器是我们的手机和平板电脑，子弹是由反社会的寡头领导的社交媒体公司。现在，我们可能有一个新的威胁在捕食年轻人：在线交易平台。
我们是一个病毒肆虐的国家，宵禁警报会发送到我们的手机上。创新已成为剥削的代名词。我们在市场中找到了慰藉，但市场并不是经济或进步的反映，而是越来越多地反映出少数公司利用技术与监管之间的差距进行套利的能力。这真让人沮丧。怎么办呢？我会查一下我的点赞、提及和股票。
另外，通过报名参加由您真心教授的七月战略冲刺活动，加入一个由全球近3000名校友组成的网络。我们G教授节目的嘉宾是进化心理学家杰弗里·米勒，他认为美德信号实际上可能是一股巨大的向善的力量。
note_en:
Addiction is the inability to stop consuming a chemical or pursuing an activity although it’s causing harm.
 I engage with almost every substance or behavior associated with addiction: alcohol, drugs, coffee, porn, sex, gambling, work, spending, devices, and social media. I’ve abused all of them, but don’t think I’m addicted. On a balanced scorecard, these substances and behaviors, abuse and all, have been a net positive in my life, even @twitter.
 Most disease and hardship for our species has been a function of scarcity — too little salt, sugar, fat, approval, safety, opportunities to mate. As a result, when we find these things, our brain produces the ultimate reward, the pleasure hormone dopamine. And it makes sense. Nature rewards behaviors that ensure the propagation of the species.
  The assembly line, processing power, and Amazon Prime have not only met the minimum thresholds for survival but created a new threat to our species: superabundance. Diabetes, income inequality, and fake news all are a function of our belief that more is better. Jeff Bezos capturing and hoarding the GDP of Norway doesn’t make sense for the species, but his instincts (fear of starvation, wielding power) reign supreme.
 Survival, propagation, and consumption should result in the next generation being smarter, faster, and stronger. Where things have come off the rails is a function of our innovation economy moving faster than our instincts. Historically, humans have engaged in activities that have natural stopping cues — the end of a chapter, the end credits. Platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and Netflix have systematically  eradicated stopping cues. Even casinos are deliberately laid out without hard angles, so it’s all one continuous space and you keep moving through it, on to the next game.
 Technological progress lapping the calibration of our instincts culminates in an endless scroll. We’re unable to find the off switch. Unlike our parents and grandparents, for us, dopamine release no longer depends on sacrifice, engagement, or grit, but on sitting still, as in 15, 14, 13 seconds until episode five of  Killing Eve will begin. There are more filtered photos, more porn, more equities, more margin, more dopa, and more time without the nuisance of needing to engage in life.
 The most recent crack dealers are online trading platforms (OTPs). What does an endless scroll look like on a trading platform?
 Gamification: Users can tap up to  1,000x per day to improve their position on the waitlist for Robinhood’s cash management feature (essentially a high-yield checking account on the app).
  Our institutions (courts, Congress, the SEC) are supposed to  slow our thinking so our reflexive instincts are checked and we can decide not to discriminate, not to pour  mercury into the rivers, and not to let a bankrupt car rental firm ( Hertz) issue shares bound to be worthless. You lose, they win.
 Technological change is vastly outpacing our species’ ability to adapt to an endless barrage of stimuli. This discrepancy in modulation has exploded our levels of teen  depression and social chaos. We are in a Supermarine Spitfire, accelerating every day, hoping the fuselage holds together as we approach the sound barrier — streaming 31 seasons of  The Simpsons, lifelike video games, ubiquitous porn of increasing extremes, high-def documentation in real time of the party your 15-year-old daughter wasn’t invited to, social media algorithms fueled on emotion vs. veracity, and immediate approval of margin for a “bull put spread.”
  I was a fucking mess yesterday after learning of the suicide of Alexander Kearns, a 20-year-old from Naperville, Illinois, who was interested in the markets and began trading stocks. Kearns mistakenly believed he was down $730,000 after trading options on the Robinhood app and took his own life. We don’t know what other factors were at play here, and young men taking their own lives after losing money in the market is not a new phenomenon.
  Facebook and Twitter do what CNN and Fox have been doing for decades, but better. I’m afraid Robinhood might become an addictive platform — Instagram for trading. Robinhood users skew young (32% of visitors are between 25–34). The firm reported 3 million new accounts in Q1 2020.  Half were first-time traders. In addition, with Vegas and sports wagering all but shut down, OTPs have become the place where emerging gambling addiction can take root and/or a rehab facility where your sponsor is a dealer.
 Learning to invest and understanding the markets are good things, as is connecting with friends online… to a point. Social media and gambling have the same addictive psychological mechanism: variable rewards (when you keep performing an action in hopes of getting a possible but unlikely reward). This is the type of behavior that’s the “most addictive and  hardest to stop.” Robinhood management and investors have taken cues from Big Tech and made a conscious decision to disregard the well-being of our youth for personal enrichment.
   12% of all trading  activity is from day-traders, yet day-traders are only 1.6% of all profitable traders.
   Investors with a large differential between their existing economic conditions and their aspiration levels hold riskier stocks in their portfolios.
 Most articles will focus on what we, Americans, view as the profound risk with the surge in rookie online traders… that the  markets might go down. Most market tops coincide with retail investors entering. We haven’t, to my knowledge, seen the scale of a market crash driven by twentysomethings  investing government rescue funds, levered up via preapproval on their smartphones.
 Our elected officials and gross idolatry of money and innovators have overrun the institutions charged with slowing our thinking and keeping our kids safe. Joe Scarborough  put it well: “Mark, Sheryl, and Jack, you have revealed yourselves to be vapid vulgarians who put at risk Americans’ health, racial justice, fair elections, and basic truths.”
 Where do we turn? The bulk of the pressure to protect kids from device addiction falls on parents — limiting use (severely) and getting other parents at school to limit use as well, so kids don’t feel they are an exception. It’s difficult, and it needs to be done. An “electronics fast,” perhaps for the whole family, can allow the nervous system to  reset. Lowering your  dopamine threshold allows a smaller amount of pleasure to be satisfying.
 The threat of addiction has been slowing our household down. One of our sons demonstrates behavior consistent with device addiction. It’s terrifying. Everything he does, says, and works toward, is in pursuit of the dopa hit waiting on his iPad. His mom and I are doing what most parents would do — reading, seeking outside help, limiting use. But more than anything, we’re trying to slow things down. Time with him, especially outdoors or with books. Time in bed with him telling him stories about his grandfather becoming a frogman in the Royal Navy. Slowing everything down. It appears to be working.
 I see Alex Kearns, and I see my oldest son. A nerd, with a big smile, fascinated by the markets and seeking dopa hits. I can’t imagine the pain of that family. I can’t imagine how we’ve lost the script, letting the meaningful—innovation and money—trump the profound: our kids. The youth suicide rate has increased by  56% in a decade. Girls between 10 and 14 had a  tripling of self-harm episodes between 2009 and 2015. Teens who are on social media for more than five hours a day are  twice more likely to be depressed than those who are on for less than an hour.
  Is it any wonder Tim Cook  doesn’t want his nephew on social media? If he wasn’t Tim Cook, would he also say, I don’t want him to have an iPad either?
 The weapons are our phones and tablets, and the bullets are social media  firms headed by sociopathic oligarchs. And now, we may have a new menace preying on young men: online trading platforms.
 We are a virus-ravaged nation where curfew alerts are sent to our phones. Innovation has become synonymous with exploitation. We find solace in the market being high, but the market is not a reflection of the economy or progress, but increasingly of the ability of a few firms to arbitrage the gap between the pace of technology and regulation. It’s depressing. What to do? I’ll check my likes, mentions, and stocks.
  P.S. Join a network of nearly 3,000 alumni worldwide by signing up for the  July Strategy Sprint, taught by yours truly. And our guest on the   Prof G Show is evolutionary psychologist Geoffrey Miller, who thinks virtue signaling could actually be a huge force for good.
title: 罗宾汉把在线交易变成了一种瘾
title_en: Robinhood Has Gamified Online Trading into an Addiction

note:
当黑洞碰撞时，考虑到这两个物体都是看不见的，随之而来的宇宙戏剧被认为是在黑暗的斗篷下上演的。但现在天文学家相信他们已经完成了这种合并的第一次光学观测，其标志是一道比太阳亮一万亿倍的火焰。
这场耀斑与去年由引力波天文台LIGO探测到的已知黑洞合并有关，LIGO接收到了通过空间结构发出的涟漪。最新的观测表明，当这些灾难性事件发生在一个更巨大的黑洞的吸积盘内时，它们会被周围的尘埃和气体照亮，使它们也能被光学望远镜看到。
“这个超大质量黑洞在这次突如其来的耀斑之前已经存在多年了，”这项工作的主要作者、加州理工学院天文学研究教授马修·格雷厄姆(Matthew Graham)说。“我们的结论是，耀斑很可能是黑洞合并的结果。”
作者并未完全排除其他来源，但纽约城市大学(City University Of New York)的合著者萨维克·福特(Saavik Ford)表示，怀疑的窗口很窄。“我们有99.9%的把握，”她说。
伯明翰大学引力波天文学研究所所长Alberto Vecchio教授表示，专家们现在将密切关注最新的观测结果与LIGO科学家将在未来几个月发表的对同一事件的详细分析。“如果两个独立的观察结果与…一致。这真的会是相当壮观的事情，“他说。
在观察之前，福特和她的同事巴里·麦克南(Barry McKernan)从理论上预测，如果黑洞合并发生在第三个超大质量黑洞的吸积盘的背景下，黑洞合并将是可见的，这与预期相反。
福特和麦科南与兹维基瞬变设施(ZTF)的项目科学家格雷厄姆合作，ZTF是一台全天巡天望远镜，旨在发现明亮的事件。福特说：“事实证明，它对这样的东西来说是完美的。”
科学家们仔细研究了Zwicky的数据，寻找与LIGO检测到的已知碰撞在时间和地点上重合的任何耀斑，LIGO在每次检测到耀斑时都会发布公共警报。有一件事格外引人注目：LIGO在去年5月检测到一项名为S190521g的合并。
格雷厄姆说：“这肯定不是我们三年前开始调查时预测到的事情之一。”
更仔细的分析表明，合并发生在一个名为J1249+3449的遥远的超大质量黑洞附近，其直径相当于地球绕太阳的轨道。这对较小的黑洞位于吸积盘的外部，一个由恒星、尘埃和气体组成的光环围绕着巨大的中央天坑旋转。福特说：“这些物体就像愤怒的蜜蜂一样聚集在中心那只可怕的蜂后周围。”
由于这对黑洞的大小约为怀特岛大小，总质量为150个太阳，向内螺旋并合并，引力波在太空中发出，新的合并后的物体受到相反方向的踢，使其穿过圆盘的尘埃和气体，向外飞入周围空间。
麦科南说：“正是气体对这颗飞驰的子弹的反应，产生了用望远镜可以看到的明亮耀斑。”
如果得到证实，这些观测可能有助于解决黑洞天文学中的一个核心问题：重量级黑洞的数量远远超过了应有的数量。黑洞是由坍塌的古老恒星形成的。当它们合并时会形成更大的黑洞，但一些黑洞太大了，从理论上说，它们滚雪球达到观测到的大小需要比宇宙年龄更长的时间。
一个潜在的解释是，如果黑洞在吸积盘中聚集在一起，那么多轮合并的可能性就会大得多。Vecchio说：“如果你有一个地方可以把这些黑洞保留在一个地方，那么你就可以有效地将它们配对。”“气体是把它们粘在一起的粘合剂。”
LIGO的观测并不容易解决这个问题，因为引力波天文学不能准确地定位合并发生在天空中的什么地方，但如果用传统望远镜也能看到同样的事件，答案可能很快就会出来。这项研究结果发表在“物理评论快报”杂志上。
note_en:
When black holes collide, the ensuing cosmic drama was assumed to play out under the cloak of darkness, given that both objects are invisible. But now astronomers believe they have made the first optical observations of such a merger, marked by a blaze of light a trillion times brighter than the sun.
 The flare was linked to a known black hole merger detected last year by the gravitational wave observatory, Ligo, which picked up ripples sent out through the fabric of space. The latest observations suggest that when these cataclysmic events occur within the accretion disk of an even more gigantic black hole, they are brilliantly illuminated by the surrounding dust and gas, making them also visible to optical telescopes.
 “This supermassive black hole was burbling along for years before this more abrupt flare,” said Matthew Graham, a research professor of astronomy at the California Institute of Technology and lead author of the work. “We conclude that the flare is likely the result of a black hole merger.”
  The authors have not entirely ruled out other sources, but Saavik Ford, a co-author based at the City University of New York, said the window of doubt was narrow. “We are 99.9% sure,” she said.
 Prof Alberto Vecchio, the director of the Institute of Gravitational Wave  Astronomy at the University of Birmingham, said experts would now be watching closely to see how the latest observations align with a detailed analysis of the same event due to be published in the coming months by Ligo scientists. “If the two independent observations line up … this would really be something rather spectacular,” he said.
 The observations came after Ford and her colleague, Barry McKernan, made theoretical predictions that black hole mergers would be visible, contrary to expectations, if they occurred against the backdrop of the accretion disk of a third supermassive black hole.
 Ford and McKernan teamed up with Graham, a project scientist for the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF), an all-sky survey telescope designed to spot bright events. “It turns out to be perfect for something like this,” said Ford.
  The scientists trawled through the Zwicky data looking for any flares that coincided in place and time with known collisions that had been detected by Ligo, which releases public alerts each time a detection is made. One event stood out: a merger referred to as S190521g that Ligo detected in May last year.
 “It’s certainly not one of the things you would have predicted three years ago when we started the survey,” said Graham.
 Closer analysis suggested the merger had taken place in the vicinity of a distant supermassive black hole called J1249+3449, with a diameter equivalent to Earth’s orbit around the sun. The pair of smaller black holes sat at the outer reaches of the accretion disk, a halo of stars, dust and gas swirling around the vast central sinkhole. “These objects swarm like angry bees around the monstrous queen bee at the centre,” said Ford.
 As the pair of black holes, each around the size of the Isle of Wight and with a combined mass of 150 suns, spiral inwards and coalesce, gravitational waves are sent out across space and the new, merged object experiences a kick in the opposite direction, sending it ploughing through the dust and gas of the disk and out into surrounding space.
  “It’s the reaction of the gas to this speeding bullet that creates a bright flare, visible with telescopes,” said McKernan.
 If confirmed, the observations could help to resolve a central problem in black hole astronomy: that there are far more heavyweight black holes than there ought to be.  Black holes form from collapsed ancient stars. Bigger black holes form when these merge, but some black holes are so big that in theory it ought to have taken longer than the age of the universe for them to snowball to their observed size.
 A potential explanation is that if black holes cluster together in accretion disks then multiple rounds of mergers become far more likely. “If you have a place where you can retain these black holes in one place, then you can pair them up efficiently,” said Vecchio. “The gas is the glue that keeps them all together.”
 Ligo observations are not easily able to resolve this question, because gravitational wave astronomy is not able to pinpoint exactly where in the sky a merger has taken place, but if the same events could be seen using conventional telescopes, an answer could be imminent. The findings are published in the journal  Physical Review Letters
title: 科学家说，黑洞可能与一万亿个太阳的光融合。
title_en: Black holes may merge with light of a trillion suns, scientists say
